lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: It seems you're missing out on some valuable experience. Would you like it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: Hello. I've found some stuff that I think is yours. You can pay me with cash you're carrying, or I can take payments from your bank. Would you like to buy anything from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you charge for returning lost items?

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: You could get your stuff back elsewhere, sure. Now, maybe it'd be free, or maybe you'd have to pay someone, but you'd definitely have to go on a journey to get it back.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: So I'm earning my fee by saving you a trip. Do you want to have a look?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Show me what you've found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About items that break when people kill me...

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: Oh yes, some items do break specially when you're killed by your rivals. And if you die in the deep Wilderness, they vanish altogether.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you fix them?

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: For a fee, yes. Just show me the broken item.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you stop them vanishing in deep Wilderness?

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: I can lock them to you so they won't vanish on death in deep Wilderness, yes. I'll want some money for my services, though, and you'll need to bring me a Trouver Parchment.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: You'll find her in Ferox Enclave, where people play a game called Last Man Standing. Bring the parchment here, then show me the item you want to lock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the parchment.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: Not bad for a human! Okay, show me the item you want to protect, and I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: I can lock that item so that it won't vanish if you die in deep Wilderness, but I'll need a Trouver parchment.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: You'll find her in Ferox Enclave, where people play a game called Last Man Standing. Bring the parchment here, then show me the item again.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: I can lock that item so that it won't vanish if you die in deep Wilderness. It'll cost you 500,000 coins and a Trouver parchment.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: I've locked that item to you so that it won't vanish if you're killed in deep Wilderness. I can unlock it again, returning your parchment and 475,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: If you want me to lock an item to you so that it won't vanish if you're killed in deep Wilderness, show me the item.

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: I have something for you. You lost your Echo boots. Would you like it back?

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: Ah! I have something for you. You lost your banner. Would you like it back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

sections.title: Deadman Mode with an active breach

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: You seem lost, but luckily I know where to send you. A breach is occurring [location 1] (single). Another breach is occuring [location 2] (multi).

lines.speaker: Perdu
lines.text: You have [time left] remaining. Otherwise...
